lines.english: Drinking it in, one becomes immortal and free of desire.
The body and mind are cooled and soothed, and the fire is extinguished.
Such a being is the embodiment of bliss, famous throughout the world. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਦੇ ਸਾਜਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tan man jeeo pindd de saajiaa |
lines.english: You blessed me, and fashioned my body, mind and soul.
I am forever a sacrifice to You, hundreds of thousands of times.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਦੇਵਉ ਜਾ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਤੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa devau jaa sabh kichh teraa |
lines.english: What can I offer You, Lord? Everything belongs to You.
By Guru's Grace, this lowly being was exalted. ||3||
As You are, so You have revealed Yourself to me.
Opening the door, You summoned me to the Mansion of Your Presence.
Says Nanak, the screen is totally torn away;
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਤੇਰਾ ਤੂ ਮੈ ਮਨਿ ਵੂਠਾ ॥੪॥੩॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: hau teraa too mai man vootthaa |4|3|14|
lines.english: I am Yours, and You are enshrined within my mind. ||4||3||14||
He has linked His servant to His service.
The Divine Guru has poured the Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord, into his mouth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਗੁਰ ਕਉ ਹਉ ਸਦ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis gur kau hau sad balihaaree |1|
lines.english: I am forever a sacrifice to that Guru. ||1||
He has subdued all his anxiety.
The True Guru has perfectly resolved my affairs.
The True Guru vibrates the unstruck melody of the sound current. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਨਰੁ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਨ ਜੋਨੀ ਪਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: so nar bahur na jonee paae |2|
lines.english: is not cast into the womb of reincarnation again. ||2||
One whose bonds are shattered by the Sovereign Lord
One whom He blesses with patience becomes blissful.
His Glory is profound and unfathomable.
One who is illuminated by the Lord's radiance within,
He holds in his robe the gems and jewels.
is not touched by pain and sorrow.
He worships and adores the One Immaculate Lord alone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਓਹੁ ਜਨੁ ਲੈ ਤਰਿਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sagal kuttanb ohu jan lai tariaa |3|
lines.english: That humble being is saved, along with all his generations. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਭਰਮੁ ਨ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਦੂਜਾ ॥
lines.translit: naa kichh bharam na dubidhaa doojaa |
lines.english: He has no doubt, double-mindedness or duality at all.
Says Nanak, I have found God, the source of nectar. ||4||4||15||
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
Wherever I look, I see the Merciful Lord.
The Will of God is dear to me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਰੈ ਸੁ ਮਨਿ ਮੇਰੈ ਮੀਠਾ ॥
lines.translit: jo kichh karai su man merai meetthaa |
lines.english: Whatever He does, seems sweet to my mind.
My self-conceit has been eliminated from my body.
And then, these eyes behold the wondrous Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੁਝਿ ਗਈ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ ਮਮਤਾ ਗੁਰਿ ਪੂਰੈ ਲੀਓ ਸਮਝਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bujh gee trisan nivaaree mamataa gur poorai leeo samajhaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: My thirst is quenched, and my attachment is dispelled. The Perfect Guru has instructed me. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਬ ਮੋਹਿ ਜਾਨੀ ਰੇ ਮੇਰੀ ਗਈ ਬਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ab mohi jaanee re meree gee balaae |
lines.english: Now, I have become wise and my demons are gone.
In His Mercy, the Guru has kept me under His protection.
The Guru has attached me to the Lord's Feet.
When the mind is totally held in check,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜੋ ਕੀਨੋ ਹਮ ਤਿਸ ਕੇ ਦਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: jo jo keeno ham tis ke daas |
lines.english: Whoever You have created, I am his slave.
one sees the Guru and the Supreme Lord God as one and the same. ||2||
I walk arm in arm, like brothers, with all. ||3||
I have no enemies, no adversaries.
My God dwells in all.
He Himself cherishes all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਗੁਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਦੀਏ ਸੂਖਾ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kau gur har dee sookhaa |
lines.english: One whom the Guru, the Lord, blesses with peace,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਉ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਨ ਲਾਗਹਿ ਦੂਖਾ ॥
lines.translit: taa kau bahur na laageh dookhaa |
lines.english: does not suffer in pain any longer.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
Nanak is imbued with the love of the Lord of the World. ||4||5||16||
You read the scriptures, and the commentaries,
You preach to others to have faith,
but the Perfect Lord does not dwell in your heart.
but you do not practice what you preach. ||1||
You place your stone god before yourself,
O Pandit, O religious scholar, contemplate the Vedas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕਾ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਨਿਵਾਰਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: man kaa krodh nivaar panddit |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Eradicate anger from your mind, O Pandit. ||1||Pause||
